description_long: This object is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain texture and a dark, weathered finish. The handle is ergonomically shaped, tapering towards the end for a secure grip, and appears to be made from a single piece of hardwood. The axe head is forged from dark metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade and a flat poll opposite the cutting edge. The blade has a single sharp edge and a pronounced beard, extending downward for increased cutting surface. The head is securely mounted onto the handle using a traditional eye and wedge method, with no visible fasteners or modern attachments. There are no visible markings, engravings, or labels on either the handle or the blade.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a dark, wedge-shaped metal blade, featuring a pronounced beard and a traditional mounting style.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, dark metal blade with a pronounced beard.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow width of the handle and the thickness of the axe head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the blade's flat poll and cutting edge.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the sharp edge of the blade and the alignment of the handle with the axe head.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural grain, oval cross-section, ergonomic grip, and a slightly tapered end.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a pronounced beard and flat poll, no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting through the eye of the blade.
